summary: Matched the fighter lipstick swing translation unit by implementing ftCo_800CDAB4 and ft_800CDB9C, updating lipstick item prototypes, correcting GALE01 split boundaries, and marking melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c as Matching. The decomp.dev report credited 2 new matches totaling +472 matched bytes and +608 linked bytes.
what_changed: Added ftCo_800CDAB4 in src/melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c. It initializes Fighter cmd_vars[0] to 540000 and cmd_vars[1] to 0, calls ftCo_800CD140, and installs ft_800CDB9C as accessory4_cb plus ft_800CD31C as take_dmg_cb.

Added ft_800CDB9C in src/melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c. The function gets the Fighter from the gobj, asserts the held item kind is It_Kind_LipStick, conditionally plays SFX, consumes throw_flags_b0, transforms a lipstick-related Vec through lb_8000B1CC, and either calls it_80295770 or spawns effect 1032 with efAsync_Spawn depending on it_8026B594.
Added required includes for effect spawning, fighter helper declarations/inlines, item forward declarations, lipstick/star rod item APIs, and lb_00B0 vector/joint helper usage.
Changed ft_800CDB9C declaration in ftlipstickswing.h from unknown return/params to void ft_800CDB9C(Fighter_GObj*).
Typed two lipstick item helper prototypes in itlipstick.h: it_80295748(Item_GObj*, Vec*) and it_80295770(Item_GObj*, Vec*, float, int).
Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c").
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt for ftlipstickswing data and sdata2 end addresses: data end 0x803C6F40 to 0x803C6F39, sdata2 end 0x804D8F18 to 0x804D8F14.
smart_moves: Promoted the translation unit only after providing typed function signatures and enough cross-module declarations for clean matching.
Used existing fighter callback fields accessory4_cb and take_dmg_cb instead of inventing new control flow for the lipstick swing behavior.
Kept item-side helper prototypes in itlipstick.h, allowing fighter code to call lipstick-specific functions with concrete Item_GObj and Vec types.
Matched behavior through existing engine helpers: GET_FIGHTER, HSD_ASSERT, ft_PlaySFX, lb_8000B1CC, it_80295748, it_80295770, and efAsync_Spawn.
Updated splits alongside the source match, indicating the data/sdata2 boundaries were remeasured as part of making the unit link as Matching.
decomp_lessons: For fighter item-action units, matching may require typing item helper APIs first; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete Item_GObj*, Vec*, float, and int parameters can unlock the fighter-side match.
Callback setup functions often initialize cmd_vars sentinel values and assign fighter callbacks immediately after delegating to a common action-state setup helper.
The command variable sentinel 540000 is significant in this implementation: it is used to prevent repeated SFX handling after a command variable has been consumed.
throw_flags_b0 can act as a one-frame trigger: this implementation clears it before performing the item/effect spawn path.
When a file becomes matching, remember to update both configure.py object status and GALE01 split ranges if data or sdata2 ownership changes.
Preserve observed ordering even when it looks unusual: ft_800CDB9C performs HSD_ASSERT on itGetKind(fp->item_gobj) before the later NULL check.
naming_conventions: Action-state callbacks in this file use ftCo_LipstickSwing_Anim, ftCo_LipstickSwing_IASA, ftCo_LipstickSwing_Phys, and ftCo_LipstickSwing_Coll.
Still-unknown address-based fighter functions retain ft_800CDB9C naming when no semantic name is established.
Common fighter functions in this area use the ftCo_800Cxxxx address-style prefix until better names are known.
Item helper names remain address-based it_80295748 and it_80295770, but their prototypes are made concrete in the item-specific header.
Enum/style evidence from the diff uses It_Kind_LipStick for the lipstick item kind.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Concrete prototypes replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for ft_800CDB9C, it_80295748, and it_80295770, improving call codegen and type correctness.
Local variables in ft_800CDB9C include two Vec temporaries and a float initialized to 0.0f before efAsync_Spawn, matching the effect spawn call shape.
The implementation preserves explicit integer temporary handling for throw_flags_b0 by assigning y to 1 or 0 before the spawn branch.
The two new matches reported by decomp.dev were ft_800CDB9C (+376 bytes) and ftCo_800CDAB4 (+96 bytes), both from main/melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.
Splits were tightened for ftlipstickswing data/sdata2, suggesting the match depended on correctly assigning object-file-owned constants/data boundaries.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported GALE01 matched code increased to 43.02% (+0.01%, +472 bytes) and linked code increased to 27.35% (+0.02%, +608 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Look for neighboring PRs matching ftstarrodswing or ft_0CDD to compare shared item-swing callback patterns.
Search for other uses of cmd_vars[0] == 540000 to understand whether this sentinel is common across item swing actions.
Search for it_80295748 and it_80295770 callers to refine semantic names for lipstick item position/spawn helpers.
Check whether the unused local variable pattern in ft_800CDB9C appears in full source after merge or was required for matching in this compiler context.
